title: Anoectochilus hatusimanus

This family is commonly known as the orchid family and comprises over 30,000 species. Anoectochilus hatusimanus is further classified within the genus Anoectochilus, which contains approximately 17 species of terrestrial orchids. The classification of Anoectochilus hatusimanus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Anoectochilus hatusimanus, also known as Hatusima's hood orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Taiwan. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as protect against cell damage. Anoectochilus hatusimanus contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
